properties.otherAdvice: Changed traffic conditions, including lane closures and reduced speed limits, will be in place at various times along Mamre Rd, and other nearby roads, as part of the Mamre Rd upgrade project.
properties.displayName: CHANGED TRAFFIC CONDITIONS Mamre Road upgrade

properties.otherAdvice: The road has been reduced to a single lane in each direction 24/7 until approximately September 2026 for work associated with the M6 Stage 1 project.

A reduced load limit on Hampden Bridge has been introduced, effective from Friday 27 June 2025.

Heavy vehicles over 23 tonnes are restricted from using the bridge and will need to plan a suitable detour.

All other vehicles will not be affected and can continue to use the bridge.

Emergency services vehicles over 23 tonnes will still be able to use the bridge at all times.

Heavy Vehicle operators of Class 1,2, and 3 vehicles travelling under notice must consult their relevant enforceable network map to confirm a suitable and approved detour route.
For sections, single lane traffic conditions will be in place. Motorists are advised to allow extra travel time.
A reduced speed limit is in place on Tantangara Rd for safety due to construction work associated with Snowy 2.0.
There is no access to Jenolan Caves.

Changes to traffic conditions will be in place on the Sydney Harbour Bridge at various times while the bridge and other infrastructure are being upgraded and maintained. These changes include a combination of lane closures and speed reductions.

At least one lane will be maintained in each direction during work hours.

Access to Cahill Exp over Circular Quay southbound may not be available at times. Motorists can detour via the city using Grosvenor St, Bridge St, and Macquarie St for access to the Eastern Suburbs and airport.
properties.displayName: CHANGED TRAFFIC CONDITIONS Sydney Harbour Bridge

properties.otherAdvice: Snowy 2.0 construction vehicles only will be allowed access.
properties.displayName: CHANGED TRAFFIC CONDITIONS Snowy 2.0 road closure

properties.otherAdvice:
Special event clearways will be in place at the following locations to facilitate Sydney Trains trackwork replacement bus operations.

  • Chalmers St, western side, between Cleveland St and Devonshire St, and
  • Chalmers St, eastern side, between Cleveland St and Rutland St.

Special event clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.
properties.displayName: SPECIAL EVENT CLEARWAYS Sydney Trains Trackwork

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip on public transport.
properties.otherAdvice:
Temporary traffic changes around the University of Wollongong and TAFE NSW will be in place from Monday 2 March to Friday 10 April, to minimise congestion for the start of the university year. 

The temporary traffic changes are as follows:

  • Traffic exiting the M1 Motorway southbound at University Ave, will be diverted left to make a U-turn at the eastern roundabout. University-bound motorists will then be detoured to the Western access route via Murphys Avenue and Robsons Road. 
  • Motorists wishing to access the M1 northbound from Keiraville and Gwynneville are encouraged to do so from Robsons Road at West Wollongong to avoid delays.

Bus access from University Avenue into Irvine Street will not be affected during diversions. Outside peak periods traffic will continue to access the University via Irvine Street and Northfields Avenue.

Traffic controllers will be on site to direct motorists.

properties.headline: HAZARD Landslide WEST OF BELLINGEN Waterfall Way between Horseshoe Road and Boggy Creek Road
properties.otherAdvice: Waterfall Way is closed until further notice. Updates about the road condition will be provided as they become available.
properties.diversions:
Light Vehicles: Summervilles Rd, Gordonville Rd, Gleniffer Rd, Wheatley St, Hammond St and Bridge St, in both directions. This route has a 10 tonne vehicle limit.

Heavy vehicles: use an alternative route such as the Oxley Hwy (check travel conditions), Gwydir Hwy or New England Hwy. 

Unless specified otherwise, operators of Class 1,2 & 3 vehicles travelling under a Notice must consult their relevant enforceable network map to confirm a suitable approved alternative route.

Operators of Class 1, 2 & 3 vehicles travelling under a Permit must not travel off the approved route listed in the permit unless an updated permit is obtained from the NHVR.

properties.otherAdvice:
The road is open with caution.

Road conditions for Unincorporated Area of the Western Region are available on 08 8082 6660.

The southbound on-ramps from Pacific Hwy, Reserve Rd, Willoughby Rd, Brook St, Miller St, Ernest St and Falcon St (Military Rd) to the Warringah Fwy southbound will also be closed.

The Sydney Harbour Tunnel will also be closed on these nights.
properties.diversions:
All southbound motorists must use the Pacific Hwy, Berry St, Arthur St and Mount St on-ramp to access the Sydney Harbour Bridge.

Motorists heading to Mosman/Manly must also use the Pacific Hwy southbound and turn left at Falcon St.

Special event clearways will be in place continuously from 2am Sat 7 Mar to 2am Mon 9 Mar the following locations to facilitate rail replacement bus operations for Metro North West trackwork:

  • Railway St, southbound between Day St & Help St,
  • Railway St, northbound between Brown St & Help St
  • Brown St, westbound between Railway St & Pacific Hwy.

No-stopping zones will also be in place northbound on Railway St between Brown St and Help St during this time.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and businesses with parking permits or TfNSW Mobility Scheme permit holders. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.21 Feb
